Otherwise, I'll have some more news for you soon.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Bev at 13:13 - Hello again. Thanks for bearing with us during the power cut.

Our staff have advised that it's going to take longer than previously anticipated to get all the remaining temporary generators connected. Power will continue to be restored in stages and our staff have advised a new time of 16:00 and 17:00 to have the final supplies back on.

I know another delay is frustrating; our engineers always try to give us the best estimate that they can but due to the safety elements of working with live electricity, sometimes they have to adjust their work at short notice.

I'm sorry if this has caused you to change any plans you've made, but we'll be here to update you until the lights are safely back on.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Hi, just an update from Bev at 12:44. Thanks for bearing with us today. Our staff are working hard to get you back on, and I just wanted to give you the latest from them. Our engineers have now connected another generator, safely restoring power to most of the affected properties, and have advised that only 13 customers around the Crittenden Road area of TN12 7 are still without power. If your power is back on now, you can stop SMS updates on our website here - https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP. Otherwise, we're sorry your lights are still off; our estimated time frame of 13:00-14:00 hasn't changed and I'll continue to keep you updated.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.

Every fault is different, so our time frames have to be estimated but currently we hope to have power back on between 12:00 and 13:00. We must stress that it may become necessary for this to change, depending on what our engineers find.

We'll keep you updated with our progress.
